L1.area: Core native image processing and pixel data manipulation library

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled string parameters are passed to an unbounded string formatting call that writes into a fixed-size buffer. When the input exceeds the expected length, the buffer overflows, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Image mode conversion and color space transformation routines in the native imaging library
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists where attacker-controlled image mode strings are formatted into a fixed-size error message buffer using an unbounded string formatting function during image mode conversion. When the mode string exceeds the expected length, the buffer overflows, potentially enabling arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: Unbounded sprintf calls with attacker-controlled mode string parameters can overflow a fixed-size static buffer in the image conversion error handling path, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.
